parameters.content:
## Turn every published social post into a Listmonk newsletter draft with PostStack

### How it works
Publish once, distribute everywhere: whenever [PostStack](https://poststack.techskills.academy) - a self-hosted social media publishing platform - publishes a post, it emits a signed `post.published` webhook. This workflow verifies the `X-PostStack-Signature` HMAC header, fetches the post's full text from the PostStack REST API, and creates a ready-to-edit **draft** campaign in [Listmonk](https://listmonk.app) - your self-hosted newsletter tool. Nothing is ever sent automatically: you open the draft, polish it, hit send.

### Setup
1. In PostStack open **Webhooks → Outbound webhooks**, click **Add endpoint** with this workflow's Production URL (the **PostStack Webhook** node) and copy the signing secret. Outbound webhooks and API access are PostStack Pro features.
2. Create an API key (**Settings → API keys**) with the posts read scope (or empty scopes for full access).
3. In Listmonk create an API user (**Admin → Settings → Users**) and note the target list's id.
4. Fill in **Configuration (EDIT ME)**: PostStack URL, API key, webhook secret, Listmonk URL, API user, token and list id.
5. See the red note on **Verify Signature** for one required n8n environment setting.

### Customization tips
Edit **Build the Draft** to change the campaign template (subject, header, footer). Prefer a weekly digest? Swap the Listmonk node for a Google Sheets append and add a scheduled workflow that compiles the week's rows into one campaign.

## Receive & Verify
Accepts the PostStack webhook and checks its HMAC signature before trusting the payload.

## Acknowledge & Filter
Responds immediately, keeps only `post.published` and builds the API lookup.

## Fetch the Post
The event carries the delivery id - resolve it to the editorial post via the posts list.

## Draft the Newsletter
Builds an HTML campaign from the post and saves it as a DRAFT in Listmonk - you always review before sending.

## Node setting required
This Code node needs `require('crypto')`. Self-hosted n8n: set `NODE_FUNCTION_ALLOW_BUILTIN=crypto` in the n8n environment, then restart.

nodes.name: PostStack Webhook
nodes.notes: Paste this node's Production URL into PostStack → Webhooks → Outbound webhooks → Add endpoint.

nodes.name: Configuration (EDIT ME)
nodes.notes: listmonkUser/listmonkToken = an API user from Listmonk -> Admin -> Settings -> Users.

nodes.name: Respond OK
nodes.notes: Acknowledge before processing - PostStack retries deliveries that don't get a 2xx within 10s.

nodes.name: Published Event?
nodes.notes: Other events are dropped - subscribe the endpoint to post.published only to keep deliveries lean.

nodes.name: Prepare Post Lookup
nodes.notes: post.published carries the delivery id, not the editorial post id - the next two nodes resolve it via the posts list.

nodes.name: Build the Draft
nodes.notes: The campaign template lives here - subject from the first line, body from the post text.

nodes.name: Create Draft Campaign in Listmonk

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ name: $json.name, subject: $json.subject, lists: [Number($('Configuration (EDIT ME)').item.json.listmonkListId)], type: 'regular', content_type: 'html', body: $json.body }) }}

nodes.notes: Creates a DRAFT - Listmonk never sends anything until you review and hit send.
